steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The blank fills "Many of whom, like neuroscience and biophysics expert Elba Serrano, ______ this mechanism." The subject is "Many" — plural. "like Elba Serrano" is a parenthetical example, not the subject.

steps.title: Rule: plural subject requires plural verb
steps.content:
Per-choice diagnosis:

A. "study" — base-form verb used with plural subjects (they study). Agrees with "Many." Correct.

B. "has studied" — singular present-perfect (he/she has studied); disagrees with plural "Many." Incorrect.

C. "studies" — singular present-tense verb (he/she studies); disagrees with plural "Many." Incorrect.

D. "is studying" — singular progressive verb; disagrees with plural "Many." Incorrect.

A is correct.
